Cordaid IM is Cordaid's AFM-regulated Dutch AIFM (registered under Article 2:66a Wft), founded 2015 in The Hague. Stability Impact Fund focuses on fragile communities. Specific fund capitalization and individual sub-fund vintages not in current public materials.

Founded 2015 by ICCO Cooperation as Truvalu (initially Fair and Sustainable Participations / iMPACT Booster). ICCO sold 50% to Boom 31 BV in 2020; ICCO merged into Cordaid Jan 2021. Fund size not publicly disclosed. INGO origin clear.

Fund Name: responsAbility Micro and SME Finance Fund
Notes: One of the largest microfinance debt managers; benchmark for ticket sizing and deployment cadence.
World's first commercial microfinance fund; now owned by Schroders. Luxembourg-domiciled SICAV — a structural precedent for INGO vehicles.
Small & Growing Businesses debt fund; Symbiotics is the sub-advisor of choice for multiple INGOs (Oxfam SEIIF, Calvert notes).
Dutch Luxembourg-SICAV microfinance fund; often cited benchmark for NAV mechanics on an open-ended INGO-adjacent structure.
Retail-accessible community-investment note; not a fund but a registered offering. Repeatedly cited by INGOs exploring direct retail capital.
Boston-based SRI manager; relevant to INGOs evaluating outsourced-CIO arrangements for endowment + impact.
Amsterdam-based VC for inclusive businesses; structural reference for venture-style INGO-adjacent funds.
Growth-stage impact PE; Temasek is a strategic LP. Sets the upper bar for fund-size that INGO-adjacent GPs aspire to.
Family-office-backed energy-transition and energy-access PE.
Bamboo + Smart Africa Alliance blended-finance vehicle. Bamboo is a go-to sub-advisor for INGO-adjacent mandates.
India-focused SMB impact PE. Part of Aavishkaar Group, one of the oldest impact managers in Asia.
India agri-tech VC. Structural comparable for INGO agri-venture teams.
Spun out of IFC in 2008; hybrid debt/equity investor in high-impact SMBs. Useful case study on IFC spin-out governance.
Series of listed bonds (WLB1-WLB6+) financing women-owned SMEs. Structural precedent for listed-outcomes vehicles.
Large-cap impact PE. Included as an "upper bound" comparable; unlikely INGO-GP competitor but often cited in LP conversations.
Quona's second fund — final close $203m on 2020-03-04. Quona spun out of Accion in 2015. Quona's first fund was the Accion Frontier Inclusion Fund ($141m final close 2018, separate entry below).
Mexico mezzanine impact fund; small-ticket structural comparable.

Fund Name: Creation Investments Social Venture Fund IV
Notes: Chicago-based inclusive-finance PE; anchor for microfinance institutions globally.
Belgium-HQ agri-inclusive-finance fund; blended-finance precedent (EIB senior, DFI mezzanine, private junior).
Women-led Latin American sustainable-business fund; precedent for gender-lens GP team composition.
UK FCDO-seeded African agri specialist. Good benchmark for blended-finance from a sovereign donor.
